title: Barcelona Pavilion
description: Planes of marble, glass and chrome composing continuous space on a travertine podium.
body: Eight cruciform chromed columns carry the roof plate, releasing every wall from load. Freed of structure, the planes of Tinian marble, golden onyx and green Alpine marble slide past one another, composing space by suggestion rather than enclosure.

Demolished after the 1929 International Exposition and rebuilt from drawings and photographs in 1986, the pavilion now exists as its own reconstruction — a precise copy of a building that was always more diagram than object.
